chunks.text: Jelani Nelson is a Professor and Chair at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office locations at 633 Soda Hall and 385 Soda Hall, with email minilek@berkeley.edu. Additional links provided are his personal homepage, dissertations, and research support pages.
chunks.question: What is Jelani Nelson's position and contact information?
chunks.answer: Professor and Chair at UC Berkeley EECS.

chunks.text: Jelani Nelson's education includes a Ph.D. in Computer Science from MIT in 2011, a M.Eng. in Computer Science from MIT in 2006, and a S.B. in Computer Science and Mathematics from MIT in 2005. His research areas are Theory (THY) and Database Management Systems (DBMS).
chunks.question: What degrees did Jelani Nelson earn from MIT?
chunks.answer: Ph.D., M.Eng., and S.B. from MIT.

chunks.text: Jelani Nelson is associated with research centers including the Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB) and SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE).
chunks.question: Which research centers is Jelani Nelson involved with?

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include the ACM SIGACT Distinguished Service Prize in 2023, ACM Eugene L. Lawler Award in 2022, ACM SIGMOD Best Paper Award in 2022, CRA-E Undergraduate Research Faculty Mentoring Award in 2022, Sloan Research Fellow in 2017, Office of Naval Research Young Investigator in 2015, NSF Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists & Engineers (PECASE) in 2014, NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) in 2014, ACM SIGMOD Best Paper Award in 2010, and IBM Pat Goldberg Memorial Best Paper Award in 2010.
chunks.question: What awards did Jelani Nelson receive in 2022?
chunks.answer: ACM Eugene L. Lawler and SIGMOD Best Paper Awards.
